Lesson Progress
5%
📍 Communication CEFR A2 ⏱ ~90 mins

Directions

Master the language of navigation — asking for, giving, and following directions confidently in everyday English situations.

📖 Vocabulary 🗣 Speaking 🎧 Listening 🤝 Communication ⭐ Review
Learning Outcomes
By the end of this lesson, you will be able to…
🗣️ Give and follow directions using prepositions and direction verbs
🗺️ Describe locations and read a town map in English
🎧 Understand spoken directions in everyday English
🌐 Use cardinal directions (N/S/E/W) in four patterns
✍️ Translate direction sentences from Vietnamese to English
🔊 Pronounce direction phrases with natural connected speech
Learning Journey
1Language InputVocabulary
→
2Guided PracticeSpeaking L1–L3
→
3Language RecognitionListening
→
4Real-world CommunicationDiscussion
→
5Reflection & ReviewFinal Review
PART 1 — GETTING READY

Vocabulary & Language Input

Build the language foundation you need before communicating.

Vocabulary — 32 items
Tap any card to reveal its example sentence.

🧭 Cardinal Directions

➡️ Direction Verbs & Prepositions

🏢 Places

Useful Phrases
Practical expressions for asking and giving directions, with usage tips and mini dialogues.
Pronunciation Focus — Connected Speech
In natural English, words connect and sounds change. Practise these to sound more fluent.
💡 Tip: The "missing" sounds are features of natural speech, not mistakes. Listen for these patterns when native speakers give directions.
Quick Practice
Reinforce today's vocabulary and phrases. Complete each exercise, then check your answers.
Practice 1 — Fill in the Blank
0 / 10
Choose the correct word from the word bank for each sentence. Complete all questions, then press Check Answers.
turn leftturn rightgo straight next tooppositebehind in front ofon your lefton your right on the corner of
Practice 2 — Multiple Choice
0 / 4
Click the correct answer for each question.
Practice 3 — Drag & Drop
0 / 10
Drag the correct word from the pool into each blank.
PART 2 — SPEAKING

Speaking

Actively use vocabulary and phrases from Part 1 — from guided translation to independent production.

Level 1 — Simple Questions
Answer these questions about YOUR neighborhood. Speak in complete sentences, then compare with a model answer. There is no single correct answer.
Level 2 — Sentence Completion
Say each sentence aloud, completing the missing parts with today's vocabulary.
Level 3 — Short Dialogue
Practice with a partner. Student A asks; Student B completes the missing lines using today's phrases. Then swap roles.
Level 4 — Independent Speaking · Start → Route → Landmarks → Destination
Use this framework to give clear, well-organised directions.
🟢 1. Start — How to begin
First, ...To start, ...
➡️ 2. Route — How to move
Go straight.Turn left/right.Go past...Cross...
🏢 3. Landmarks — What you can see
You will see a...Next to...Opposite...
📍 4. Destination — Where you finish
It's next to...On your left/right.You'll arrive at...
Practice — Translate to English
Read the Vietnamese passage and speak/write the English version using the template above.
✅ Speaking Checklist
Four Ways to Give a Direction
Expand your vocabulary with North, South, East, and West patterns.
N
S
E
W
NE
NW
SE
SW
🧭
Pattern 1 · in the north/south/east/west of
The park is in the north of the city.
The beach is in the south of the country.
Pattern 2 · on the north/south/east/west side of
The park is on the north side of the city.
The hospital is on the east side of the street.
Pattern 3 · in the northern/southern/eastern/western part of
The park is in the northern part of the city.
The lake is in the western part of the park.
Pattern 4 · to the north/south/east/west of
The park is to the north of the city.
The café is to the south of the school.
Practice — Use All 4 Patterns
For each place, write all four direction patterns. Click "Show Answer" to check.
PART 3 — LISTENING

Let's Listen

Recognise direction language you have already practised, in natural spoken English.

Listen 3 — Match the Conversations
Click a place on the left, then click its matching direction statement on the right.
🎧 Listen to the six conversations (or read the transcript after finishing), then match each place with the correct directions.
Places
Statements
Tapescript — Listen 3
📚 Vocabulary Review — recycled in Listen 3: go straight up · turn left / right on · turn right onto · walk up / down · on the corner of · on your left / right · I'm looking for…
Listen 4 — Part 1: Who Talks?
Select who gives directions in each conversation and type the item they ask you to bring.
🎧 Your teacher will play the audio for Listen 4. "Who talks?" can only be heard, not read — complete this column while listening.
ConversationWho gives directions?Pickup Item Mentioned
Listen 4 — Part 2: Fill in the Blanks
Complete each sentence. Write NO MORE THAN ONE WORD per blank.
Tapescript — Listen 4
📚 Vocabulary Review — recycled in Listen 4: walk past · blocks · cross · across from · go through the intersection · take the first street · turn right · on your left
PART 4 — DISCUSSION

Mongo Land & Real-World Directions

Apply today's language to a real theme park map and real-life translation tasks.

Mongo Land Map — Đà Lạt
Use this map for all scenarios below. 35 numbered locations.
🇬🇧 Role-Play — Give Directions
English Scenarios
Use landmarks, direction verbs, and sequence words (First, Next, Then, Finally). Click "Show Sample Answer" to compare.
💭 Discussion Reflection
Which scenario was the most difficult for you? What language would help you next time?
🇻🇳 Translation Practice
Vietnamese → English
Translate each Vietnamese passage into natural English using today's vocabulary.

Consolidate everything with a matching game and a bingo challenge.

Click a word, then click its definition. Complete all 10 pairs.

Put the Route in Order

Game Over!

Here's how you did.

✍️ Writing Review — Your Own Directions
Write Directions from Your Home
Write 3–5 sentences giving directions from your home to your school or workplace.
✅ Use at least: one sequence word (First / Next / Then / Finally) · two direction verbs (go straight, turn left…) · one preposition of place (next to, opposite, behind…).
Sample Answer
First, go out of my house and turn right. Next, go straight along Nguyen Trai Street for two blocks. Then, cross the street at the traffic lights. My school is next to the post office — you can't miss it!

CEFR LevelA2 — Elementary
Vocabulary Learned32 words & phrases (cardinal directions, direction verbs, prepositions, places)
Grammar FocusImperatives · Prepositions of place · Cardinal direction patterns
Pronunciation FocusConnected speech · Unreleased T/K · Weak forms · Glide insertion
Communication FunctionsAsking for directions · Giving directions · Describing locations
Skills PracticedSpeaking · Listening · Reading · Writing · Translation
Interaction Types UsedFlip cards · Fill-in-blank · MCQ · Drag & drop · Matching · Ordering · Role-play · Dialogue practice · Bingo game · Free writing
